title: Bridge Essay: Heart-brain axis dysregulation in PTSD mice: Vagal-mediated insular cortex hyperactivity and its reversal by propranolol.
concept: Spiritus as interoceptive inference — heart-brain coupling, energy metabolism as felt presence.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Body-energy** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Spiritus as interoceptive inference — heart-brain coupling, energy metabolism as felt presence.

**Heart-brain axis dysregulation in PTSD mice: Vagal-mediated insular cortex hyperactivity and its reversal by propranolol.** - European journal of pharmacology (2026). DOI: 10.1016/j.ejphar.2026.178961. PMID: 42107745. BACKGROUND: While abnormal brain connectivity is central to PTSD, sympathetically-driven cardiac dysfunction also occurs. Given the established role of cardiac modulation of brain function and its implication in psychiatric disorders, this study aims to delineate the role of the heart-brain connection in the mechanisms underlying PTSD.
